summary: Tested the new scorer role + cross-company permission scoping batch. 24/24 backend tests passed and a frontend smoke confirmed gating, invite UI, and Scorers tab. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3,818 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend continuing the routes/* extraction (scorer endpoints are still inline at server.py:1180-1342; would fit naturally in routes/scorers.py).

POST /api/events/{event_id}/scorers returns the temp_password in plaintext in the JSON response. This is by design (SendGrid fallback) but the response is also visible in browser devtools / proxy logs — consider showing it only inline in the UI modal and clearing it from API responses once an out-of-band delivery channel (SMS/email) is proven reliable.
scorer_my_events (server.py:1316) returns {'events': []} for non-scorer roles silently. An HR hitting this endpoint will get an empty list with no error — fine functionally but a 403 may be clearer.

_can_score_fixture allows any user.role == 'scorer' who is in event_scorers. Existing non-scorer roles invited via POST /scorers (e.g., players) get an event_scorers row but their role stays e.g. 'player' — the function then returns False for them, so they silently cannot score. Either reject invites for non-scorer existing roles or expand the role check.

success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke (HR view + Admin view + invite flow)

test_credentials: Platform admin admin@kreedanation.com / admin123 used. Other accounts (Company A HR, Company B HR, scorers) seeded directly in MongoDB inside the test fixtures and cleaned up at teardown.
seed_data_creation: Each backend test run creates two TEST_CO_* companies, two HR users, one event with 4 teams. Cleaned up automatically. Frontend smoke seeded a single UI_TEST_CO + event and cleaned up after.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 review-request scenarios verified end-to-end. Backend permission tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_scorer_and_permissions.py (24 tests, ~7s). Run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL+MONGO_URL+DB_NAME env vars. The scorer email invite triggers a real SendGrid email; the temp_password also returns inline. The Scorers tab and Generate-fixtures button are correctly gated by canManage. Cross-company HR cannot generate fixtures (403) and cannot score (403). Fixture-lock correctly returns 400 once a fixture is 'live'. Per-fixture scope (scope='specific' with fixture_ids) correctly blocks scoring outside the assigned list including cricket/* endpoints.
tested_scenarios: Cross-company HR (Co B) cannot generate fixtures for Co A's event (403, 'Only the event organiser')
Owner HR (Co A) can generate fixtures
Fixture-lock: PATCHing status=live then re-calling generate-fixtures returns 400 'locked'
/cricket/setup: 403 for unrelated HR, 200 for owner HR
POST /events/{id}/scorers — creates scorer user (role=scorer), returns temp_password, assignment_id, user_created=true
GET /events/{id}/scorers — 403 for non-organiser, 200 list for organiser
GET /scorers/me/events lists event + fixtures with scope='all' or 'specific'
Scorer (scope=specific) — PATCH target OK, PATCH other 403, cricket/setup other 403
UI: HR sees Generate-fixtures button (enabled) + Scorers tab; Admin sees same
UI: Scorers tab invite form (scorer-invite-email/name/submit) creates row and shows scorer-invite-result inline with temp password
